tables.description: This subject phenotype data table includes variables of sociodemography (n=6 variables; age, sex, ancestry), bleeding history such as types of bleeding (n=6 variables) and bleeding related to surgery (n=7 variables), menstrual cycle and birth control use for female participants (n=4 variables), normal physically observable human traits (n=34 variables), blood measurements (n=20 variables), cigarette use (n=1 variable), and immunization/medications (n=2 variables).

variables.name: 5th digit clinodactyly
variables.description: Does the tip of your little finger (5th digit) bend toward your other fingers?

variables.name: ADAMTS13 IU/dL)
variables.description: ADAMTS13 antigen level in International Units per deciliter

variables.name: ATIII (mg/dL)
variables.description: Antithrombin III antigen level in milligrams per deciliter

variables.name: Acne
variables.description: Many people have some acne during their teenage years.  Which picture best describes your acne as a teenager?

variables.name: Age
variables.description: Participant age at enrollment

variables.name: Ancestry Description
variables.description: Self reported description of ethnicity

variables.name: Ancestry Description 2
variables.description: Self report description of ethnicity if more than two

variables.name: Ancestry Type
variables.description: Self reported ethnicity

variables.name: Ancestry-2
variables.description: Self reported ethnicity if more than one

variables.name: Aphthous ulcers
variables.description: How often do you get aphthous ulcers (canker sores)?

variables.name: Athletic ability
variables.description: How would you describe your athletic ability?

variables.name: Bleeding treatment with Tooth extraction
variables.description: When you had your tooth pulled (or had other dental surgery), did your doctor or you have to do something to stop the bleeding?

variables.name: Bleeding treatment with surgery
variables.description: Has your doctor or you ever had to do something to stop bleeding after surgery?

variables.name: Bleeding treatment, non-surgical
variables.description: Have you ever had medical treatment to stop bleeding that was not associated with surgery?  This includes incidents when you may have injured yourself, received vaccinations or injections, or had any part of your body pierced.

variables.name: Bleeding with Surgery
variables.description: Have you ever had problems with bleeding following any surgery?

variables.name: Bleeding with Tooth extraction
variables.description: When you had your tooth pulled (or had other dental surgery), did you have a problem with too much bleeding?

variables.name: Brain freeze
variables.description: Do you experience a brief intense headache if you drink an icy beverage or eat ice cream too fast? AKA "brain freeze"

variables.name: Cigarette smoke
variables.description: Do you currently smoke cigarettes?

variables.name: Comparative bleeding
variables.description: Compared to other people, when you get a minor cut or nose bleed, do you think that you bleed less, about the same, or more?  Please consider the amount of bleeding and the length of time you bleed.

variables.name: Comparative dental bleeding
variables.description: When you go to the dentist, about how much do you bleed from your gums?  Would you say it is more, about the same, or less than most people your age?

variables.name: Consent Time
variables.description: Elapsed time between online informed consent document page loading and clicking "Agree" button

variables.name: Dental caries
variables.description: How many dental cavities have you ever had?

variables.name: Dimples
variables.description: Do you have dimples on your face?

variables.name: Dyslexia
variables.description: Do you have dyslexia (difficulty with reading)?

variables.name: Ear lobe attachment
variables.description: Which word best describes your ear lobes -- free or attached?

variables.name: Ear lobe creased
variables.description: Are your ear lobes creased?

variables.name: Eating Sweets
variables.description: How much do you like to eat sweets?

variables.name: Eating salty food
variables.description: How much do you like to eat salty foods?

variables.name: Eye color
variables.description: Which picture best describes your natural eye color?

variables.name: Family Id
variables.description: De-identified sibship ID number

variables.name: Favorite color
variables.description: Of the following colors, which is your favorite color?

variables.name: Flat feet
variables.description: Do you have flat feet?

variables.name: Freckles
variables.description: Which picture best describes the amount of freckles you have?

variables.name: Gamma Fibrinogen (IU/dL)
variables.description: Gamma prime fibrinogen antigen level in International Units per deciliter

variables.name: Geneva Id
variables.description: De-identified Geneva consortium subject ID number

variables.name: Gum Bleeding
variables.description: When you brush your teeth how often do you notice bleeding from your gums?

variables.name: HCT (%)
variables.description: Hematocrit, percent of blood that is contained in the red blood cell pellet

variables.name: HGB (g/dL)
variables.description: Hemoglobin concentration in grams per deciliter of whole blood

variables.name: Hair color
variables.description: Which picture best describes your natural hair color (prior to graying)?

variables.name: Hair line
variables.description: Which picture best describes your current hair line?

variables.name: Handedness
variables.description: With which hand do you write best?

variables.name: Height
variables.description: Participant height at time of registration

variables.name: Hormonal birth control
variables.description: Do you use hormonal birth control (pill, patch, ring, or shot)?

variables.name: Immunization
variables.description: Have you been immunized (received a vaccine shot) in the last month?

variables.name: Interlocking fingers
variables.description: Interlock your fingers as shown in the picture.  Which position feels comfortable to you?

variables.name: Lifetime nosebleed
variables.description: How many spontaneous (that seem to happen for no reason) nosebleeds would you estimate you have had in your entire life?

variables.name: MCH (pg)
variables.description: Mean corpuscular hemoglobin in picograms

variables.name: MCHC (g/dL)
variables.description: Mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration in grams per deciliter

variables.name: MCV (fL)
variables.description: Mean corpuscular volume in femtoliters

variables.name: MPV (fL)
variables.description: Mean Platelet Volume

variables.name: Medications
variables.description: Please check all the medications listed below that you have used more than twice in the past month.

variables.name: Menarche
variables.description: At what age did you have your first period?

variables.name: Menstrual duration
variables.description: On average, how many days do your periods last?

variables.name: Menstrual heaviness
variables.description: How frequently do you change pads/tampons on the heaviest days of your periods?

variables.name: Migraines
variables.description: Have you been diagnosed with migraines by a physician?

variables.name: Nosebleeds per year
variables.description: How many spontaneous (that seem to happen for no reason) nosebleeds would you estimate you have per year?

variables.name: PLG (IU/dL)
variables.description: Plasminogen antigen level in International Units per deciliter

variables.name: PLT (x10E03cells/microliter)
variables.description: Platelet cell count per microliter of whole blood

variables.name: Photic sneeze
variables.description: Do you often feel like sneezing or sneeze upon walking out into bright sunlight?

variables.name: Protein C (IU/dL)
variables.description: Protein C antigen level in International Units per deciliter

variables.name: Protein S (IU/dL)
variables.description: Protein S antigen level in International Units per deciliter

variables.name: RBC (x10E06cells/microliter)
variables.description: Red blood cell count per microliter of whole blood

variables.name: RDW (%)
variables.description: Red blood cell width distribution, variance in percentage

variables.name: Seasonal allergies
variables.description: Do you have itchy eyes, runny or stuffy nose, or sneezing in the fall or spring?

variables.name: Sex
variables.description: Participant gender

variables.name: Shoe Size
variables.description: What is your US shoe size?

variables.name: Skin color
variables.description: Which best describes the color of the skin on the inside of your upper arm?  In other words, what is your natural skin color without any tanning?

variables.name: Skin sensitivity to sun
variables.description: What would happen to your skin if it were exposed to bright sunlight for the first time in summer for one hour at noon without protection?  Would your skin become:

variables.name: Skin tags
variables.description: Skin tags are small, normal extra pieces of skin most commonly found on the neck or under arm.  How many skin tags do you have?

variables.name: Spontaneous Bruising
variables.description: How often do you bruise without trauma or injury?  In other words, how often do you get bruises that seem to happen for no reason?

variables.name: Stuttering speech
variables.description: Have you ever been treated for stuttering by a speech therapist?

variables.name: Surgery
variables.description: Have you ever had surgery or any other operation not including dental surgery?

variables.name: Tanning
variables.description: What would happen to your skin if it was repeatedly exposed to bright sunlight without protection? Would your skin become:

variables.name: Tongue roll
variables.description: Can you roll your tongue?

variables.name: Tooth extraction
variables.description: Have you ever had a tooth extracted (pulled out) or any other dental surgery?

variables.name: VWF Std Dev
variables.description: Standard deviation of von Willebrand Factor antigen level

variables.name: VWF V2 (%)
variables.description: Von Willebrand Factor antigen level, percentage compared to pooled laboratory standard.  Updated assay compared to VWF(%).

variables.name: VWF(%)
variables.description: Von Willebrand Factor antigen level, percentage compared to laboratory standard

variables.name: VWFpp2 (IU/dL)
variables.description: Von Willebrand Factor propeptide antigen level in International Units per deciliter.  AlphaLISA using mAb 242.2 and 242.6

variables.name: Vision correction
variables.description: Do you wear prescription eye glasses or contact lenses?

variables.name: Vulcan greeting
variables.description: Can you easily separate your fingers as shown in this picture?

variables.name: WBC (x10E03cells/microliter)
variables.description: White blood cell count per microliter of whole blood

variables.name: Weight
variables.description: Participant weight at time of registration

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes body site were sample was collected, analyte type, histological type, DNA source, and genotyping center.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type used for Alphalisa

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: DNA_SOURCE
variables.description: Source of DNA used for genotyping

variables.name: GENOTYPING_CENTER
variables.description: Center where genotyping was performed

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type

variables.name: SAMPID
variables.description: Sample genotyping instance ID

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: GENEVA ID

tables.description: This pedigree data table contains subject, father and mother IDs, gender information of study participants, and twin IDs.

variables.name: FAMID
variables.description: Family ID

variables.name: FATHER
variables.description: Father ID

variables.name: MOTHER
variables.description: Mother ID

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Subject's sex

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: Subject ID (GENEVA ID)

variables.name: TWINID
variables.description: Twin ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping file contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample aliases and sample use.

variables.name: SAMPID
variables.description: Sample genotyping instance ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample Use

variables.name: SAMP_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where samples originate

variables.name: SOURCE_SAMPID
variables.description: Sample ID used in the Source Repository

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: GENEVA ID

tables.description: This subject consent file contains a listing of subjects, their consents, and mapping of subjects that are used as genotyping controls to their respective subject ID from the source repository (Coriell).

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: GENEVA ID

variables.name: SUBJ_SOURCE
variables.description: Defines subject ID name space origin
